name: John Gemignani
education: Bachelor of Science (B.S.), Computer Science from University of California, Santa Cruz; Transferred to UCSC, Mathematics and Computer Science from Cabrillo College; Programming & Linux/Unix Programming and Administration Certificates with Honors, Java, C++, Android Application development, Linux Administration and Programming from UCSC Extension; None, Unix Administration from HP Educational Services, Mountain View, CA; AA/AS/Cert, Computer Science, Electronic Technology from Cabrillo College
headline: Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft
about:
Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, focused on database engineering with an emphasis on PostgreSQL internals and graph data processing.

I’m a PMC member and long‑time core contributor to Apache AGE, an open‑source PostgreSQL extension that brings graph database capabilities and openCypher queries to PostgreSQL. I’ve worked on the project across its lifecycle—from early architecture through multiple major releases—contributing to the Cypher query engine, planner/optimizer, system catalog, drivers, and build infrastructure.

My recent work centers on keeping AGE production‑ready as PostgreSQL evolves: advancing version support, shipping releases, fixing correctness and stability issues, enhancing performance, tightening security, and maintaining CI and test pipelines.

I care deeply about making complex systems understandable and reliable. Debugging is my favorite part of the job, and I enjoy pairing that work with steady, thoughtful collaboration in open‑source communities.
Based in Santa Cruz. B.S. in Computer Science (cum laude), UC Santa Cruz.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Windows and Linux C and Java programmer. With a tiny bit of C++ and Python woven in.
Responsible for porting functionality between Windows and Linux environments. 
Designed and implemented new modules and features for core business applications.
Created stress tests for core business license servers.

experiences.title: Android Developer Consultant
experiences.jobDescription:
Just completed an Android project (NDA) that spanned the mobile application, backend server, and a consumer hardware device.

The mobile application involved working on the UI, business logic, and implementing secure web sockets with authentication.  

The backend server, which was an embedded Linux board running Node.js and MySQL, involved integrating in SSL and authentication.  

The consumer hardware device involved reverse engineering the current prototype and creating more prototypes from it.

experiences.title: Sr. Infra Subject Matter Expert (Infrastructure Programmer and Developer Support)
experiences.jobDescription: Infrastructure programmer, production and developer support.  Responsible for the creation, configuration, setup, troubleshooting and maintenance of production and development system operational environments, operational databases, and software.  Provided on call support.  Wrote programs and scripts for querying, updating, populating, and maintaining the operational Oracle databases.  Wrote programs for creating environments, monitoring production and development environments, and performing automated software distributions.

experiences.title: Sr. Subject Matter Expert (Sr. Computer Operator)
experiences.jobDescription: Sr. Computer Operator for Enterprise VMS Clusters and HP-UX Superdome.

recommendationsReceived.name: Mani Kasturi
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Taking Time Off
recommendationsReceived.context: Mani worked with John on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I know John from May 2001 at our Pleasanton offices on Owens Drive (until April 2005) and Black Avenue, under PacBell Directory Operations, SBC Directory Operations, and Amdocs IT services (supporting AT&T Yellow Pages IT) names, worked with him in various IT IS projects until he left us in March 2010, to pursue his dreams. 

He got very deep understanding of UNIX and VMS operating systems and their internals and very good at scripting languages in these OS environments. He specialized and got niche on scheduler map preparations for night operation cycles for production, production readiness test, user acceptance test, system test, and user training environments, he automated most of these operations and played major key role in outsourcing these operations to India, successfully. 

I know him personally as very good person, friend and I wish him the very best for his future endeavors, excellent future.

recommendationsReceived.name: Vivek Arora
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior SAS Infrastructure Administrator
recommendationsReceived.context: Vivek worked with John on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I've know John for a little over 5 years now. I have known him to be one of the most helpful people around. 
He is very thorough in his work and is exceptional at scripting.
John has a very keen eye for automation and is always looking out for opportunities for automating processes.
I personally have learnt a lot from John in the Infrastructure Programming domain.
Wish him all the best in everything he does.. 

Cheers,
Vivek

recommendations.name: Muhammad Taha Naveed
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer
recommendations.description:
Taha is an amazingly bright and promising individual. He is also a very quick study and a dedicated employee. 

For our project, Apache AGE, he was able to take on the core C codebase, the CI integration and associated scripts, Github, Dockerhub, issues, bugs, and features with the upmost professionalism. Any task that he was given was always very thoroughly executed. I was always impressed at just how many vastly different tasks that he could easily master in a short period of time.

Additionally, Taha takes critiques and conducts himself professionally. From meetings, to reviews, and team interactions, he always acted professionally.

Having worked with Taha for the past few years, both as his manager and as a fellow software engineer, I would highly recommend him, without hesitation, for any software development position.

summary: John Gemignani is currently starting a new position as Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, a large public technology company. This announcement-focused post indicates he shares career milestones and professional updates, typically concise and celebratory in tone. The style is personal and straightforward rather than deeply technical or long-form thought leadership. By taking on a senior engineering role at Microsoft he signals experience and capability in software engineering and working within enterprise-scale product contexts. Although this single post contains no technical detail, it serves to build professional brand, expand his network, and attract engagement from peers and recruiters. His immediate value proposition is credibility through association with a major tech company combined with clear, approachable career communication.
